query: AWS Direct Connect MACsec encryption support

metrics.content:
**AWS Direct Connect supports MACsec** for Layer 2 encryption on **dedicated Direct Connect connections**, typically **10 Gbps and 100 Gbps** ports at supported locations. It encrypts traffic only across the physical DX link between your router and the AWS Direct Connect device.

Key points:

| Area | Support |
|---|---|
| **Connection type** | Dedicated connections only |
| **Hosted connections** | Not supported |
| **Encryption scope** | Layer 2 link encryption on the DX circuit |
| **Traffic covered** | All VIF traffic on that physical connection |
| **Keying** | MACsec CKN/CAK configured between your device and AWS |
| **Device requirement** | Customer router must support MACsec and compatible cipher suites |
| **End-to-end encryption** | Not provided; use TLS/IPsec/app encryption if required beyond the DX link |

MACsec is useful when you need encryption without the throughput and operational overhead of IPsec tunnels, especially for high-bandwidth private connectivity. Verify current speed, location, and cipher support in the official AWS Direct Connect MACsec documentation before ordering or enabling it.
